title: Stop alerting on hosts that no longer exist
description: Prevent stale alerts for hosts that have been removed or no longer exist. Investigate Mac pools as a known example and ensure alert evaluation distinguishes active hosts from historical records.

title: Redesign and simplify the web UI
description: Review the current web UI and redesign it for clearer hierarchy, navigation, and presentation. Define the highest-friction areas before implementation and keep the redesign grounded in common pool-classification workflows.

title: Add a task classification preview tool
description: Add a tool where a user can provide a task and see how the current filters would classify it. Ideally support before-and-after comparison when filters are modified; a current-classification-only preview is an acceptable first version.

title: Replace oldest-data field with latest-data information
description: Evaluate and replace the existing oldest-data field with a latest-data field, which is expected to be more useful for understanding freshness. Update relevant UI and API surfaces consistently.

title: Reduce main-page query count
description: Profile the main page's data requests and consolidate them into fewer calls. Preserve existing behavior while reducing query overhead and improving page-load efficiency.

title: Show an explicit empty state when heatmap data is unavailable
description: When no heatmap data is available, display a clear no-data message instead of rendering an unexplained empty block.

title: Add utilization-over-time API for rightsizing
description:
Add a bucketed utilization-over-time API for pool rightsizing. The API must make inefficient capacity visible, such as 11 hardware-reference workers producing only about 2 worker-equivalents of actual usage.

Metric definitions:
- busy_worker_seconds = sum of task run intervals, clipped to the requested time window or bucket
- available_worker_seconds = integral over time of workers considered available
- utilization_pct = busy_worker_seconds / available_worker_seconds * 100
- worker_equivalents = busy_worker_seconds / window_seconds, representing average concurrent busy workers

Task runtime collection:
- Capture and persist the Taskcluster run resolved timestamp in addition to run_started.
- Use terminal run intervals to calculate busy time.
- Handle intervals crossing bucket boundaries by clipping them to each bucket.

Availability and capacity decisions:
- Do not maintain configured capacity manually; it is expected to drift.
- Derive availability from Taskcluster worker contact data.
- Initially consider a worker available when its last contact is within 60 minutes and it is not quarantined.
- Make the contact threshold configurable and tune it using observed last-contact ages for known healthy workers.
- Record availability transitions rather than periodic pool snapshots.
- For an offline transition, use last_contact + threshold as effective_at.
- For an online transition, use the new last_contact as effective_at.
- Quarantine and unquarantine changes must produce equivalent availability transitions.
- Transition-based storage must correctly represent a worker that disappears and later returns, while keeping storage proportional to state changes rather than polling frequency.

API expectations:
- Accept a selected time range and bucket size.
- Return pool and bucket timestamps, busy worker-hours, available worker-hours, average busy workers or worker-equivalents, utilization percentage, and data coverage metadata.
- Define utilization against available, non-quarantined workers rather than a manually configured or raw retained-worker count.
- Return null or an explicit unavailable status when the denominator is zero.

Coverage and completeness:
- Report collection_started, coverage_pct, and complete for the requested range.
- Startup incompleteness naturally disappears once the entire requested window is after collection began.
- Collection outages or missed recent-task data can create later coverage gaps, so completeness must be calculated rather than assumed.
- Avoid presenting incomplete values as authoritative rightsizing data.

Relationship to pool-classifier-49f:
- Keep job counts and jobs per active worker as separate throughput metrics. They complement utilization but cannot substitute for duration-weighted busy time.

Acceptance criteria:
- Schema migrations support resolved task times and historical availability transitions.
- Collection updates both Postgres and SQLite storage implementations with tests.
- Unit tests cover task and availability intervals crossing bucket boundaries, online/offline/return transitions, quarantine transitions, zero capacity, incomplete coverage, and configurable contact thresholds.
- API integration tests verify filtering, bucketing, formulas, stable JSON fields, and invalid parameter handling.
- API documentation states the formulas and availability semantics.
close_reason: Completed the utilization-over-time stack: task intervals, Taskcluster-derived availability transitions, coverage tracking, bucketed aggregation, versioned API, integration tests, and documentation.

title: Ignore all -vms pools by default
description: Change default filtering so every pool whose name matches the -vms convention is ignored automatically, rather than maintaining exclusions one pool at a time. Confirm the matching rule and provide an override if appropriate.

title: Add 24-hour job activity metrics
description: Expose jobs in the last 24 hours and jobs per active worker in the last 24 hours. Determine whether these belong on the summary screen, in the pool-page summary, or in a dedicated pool-page section.

title: Improve unknown-data classification and explain classification behavior
description: Classify more currently unknown data, review existing classifications for correctness, and improve the web-facing explanation of how classification works now that the functionality is available through the web UI.

title: Persist complete task-run intervals
description: Capture and persist Taskcluster run resolution times so utilization can use duration-weighted busy time. Add run_resolved to the SQLite and Postgres schemas and storage interfaces, pass the resolved timestamp through terminal-task collection, and ensure distinct task runs are not collapsed when retries occur. Include migrations and tests for completed, failed, exception, retried, and missing-timestamp cases.
close_reason: Persisted resolved task-run timestamps and distinct retry intervals across SQLite and Postgres, with migrations and tests.

title: Record worker availability transitions from Taskcluster
description: Derive worker availability from Taskcluster contact and quarantine data without manually configured capacity. Start with a configurable 60-minute recent-contact threshold. Persist online, contact-timeout, quarantine, unquarantine, and return transitions with logical effective times and observation timestamps. Use last_contact plus the threshold for offline transitions and the new contact time for online transitions. Keep storage proportional to state changes, support workers that disappear and later return, implement both SQLite and Postgres paths, and test threshold and transition edge cases.
close_reason: Added configurable Taskcluster-derived worker availability state and transition history for online, timeout, quarantine, unquarantine, disappearance, and return cases across SQLite and Postgres.

title: Track utilization data coverage intervals
description: Track whether task-run and worker-availability collection continuously cover a requested period. Persist coalesced coverage intervals rather than a row for every poll, separately identify task-run and availability coverage, start a new interval after a collection gap, and expose enough information to calculate collection_started, coverage_pct, and complete. Implement SQLite and Postgres storage with tests for startup accumulation, uninterrupted collection, outages, and resumed collection.
close_reason: Added coalesced task-run and worker-availability coverage intervals, failure/gap detection, coverage summaries, SQLite/Postgres storage, and startup/outage/resumption tests.

title: Implement bucketed utilization aggregation
description: Implement storage queries and domain logic for utilization over a selected time range and bucket size. Clip task-run and availability intervals at request and bucket boundaries. Produce busy_worker_hours, available_worker_hours, worker_equivalents, utilization_pct, and coverage metadata. Return null or an explicit unavailable value when available time is zero, and do not present incomplete data as authoritative. Cover overlapping intervals, boundary conditions, quarantine, returning workers, empty buckets, zero denominators, and incomplete coverage in unit tests for both storage backends.
close_reason: Implemented bucketed utilization aggregation with clipped task and availability intervals, coverage gating, zero-denominator handling, SQLite/Postgres queries, and boundary/overlap/quarantine/return tests.

title: Expose and document the utilization-over-time API
description: Add the Flask JSON endpoint for bucketed pool utilization. Validate selected time ranges and bucket sizes and return stable fields for pool and bucket timestamps, busy worker-hours, available worker-hours, worker-equivalents, utilization percentage, collection start, coverage percentage, and completeness. Add API integration tests for filtering, bucketing, invalid parameters, zero availability, and incomplete data. Document formulas, units, Taskcluster-derived availability semantics, the configurable contact threshold, and the distinction from job-throughput metrics in pool-classifier-49f.
close_reason: Added the versioned Flask utilization API with bounded parameter validation, stable JSON fields, SQLite/Postgres integration coverage, and complete formula/availability/coverage documentation.

title: Fix Postgres storage parity regressions
description: Fix unclassified-log deletion so it uses the active PostgresStorage transaction, add deterministic tie ordering to top_offenders on both backends, and ignore the local Postgres pgdata bind mount. Verify the full SQLite/Postgres/Flask test matrix.
close_reason: Fixed Postgres log deletion transaction visibility, deterministic top-offender tie ordering, and local pgdata ignores; verified 69 tests with Postgres enabled.

title: Support listed availability for wake-on-dispatch pools
description:
Problem

All workers under the proj-autophone provisioner are wake-on-dispatch. Unlike normal workers, they do not resume Taskcluster contact after reboot until a task is dispatched. The current recent-contact policy marks them unavailable after WORKER_CONTACT_THRESHOLD_SECONDS, causing idle Android capacity to disappear from the denominator and utilization to be overstated.

Design

Add an explicit availability_mode setting to pool configuration with signal-based values:

- recent_contact: current behavior. A worker is available only while lastDateActive is within the configured contact threshold and the worker is not quarantined.
- listed: a worker returned by a successful complete listWorkers observation is treated as eligible capacity while not quarantined, regardless of lastDateActive. A worker absent from a successful complete listing becomes unavailable at observation time.

Configure every proj-autophone pool as listed. Keep recent_contact as the default for other provisioners. Implement this through configuration rather than hard-coding the provisioner name.

Semantics and limitation

For listed mode, available means eligible listed pool capacity, not confirmed live or healthy capacity. Taskcluster provides no signal that distinguishes a dormant wake-on-dispatch Android worker from a physically dead worker that remains listed. Known-bad devices must be quarantined or supplied by a future external health source; otherwise they remain in the availability denominator and can understate utilization. Task-run intervals remain the source of busy time.

API and history

Return availability_mode in utilization API responses so consumers can interpret the denominator. Define a safe cutover for already collected proj-autophone availability history so contact-derived intervals are not silently reported as listed-mode history. This may use an explicit coverage boundary, history reset procedure, or versioned semantics, but must prevent incompatible history from appearing authoritative.

Documentation and live UI

Update the utilization documentation to define both modes and clearly state the listed-mode limitation. Live pages must identify listed-mode pools and explain that availability is based on Taskcluster listing plus quarantine state, not confirmed device liveness. At minimum, surface this on the dashboard and individual pool detail pages without implying that listed workers are verified online.

Acceptance criteria

1. Pool configuration accepts and validates recent_contact and listed; recent_contact is the default.
2. All proj-autophone pools explicitly use listed.
3. Listed workers remain available despite stale or missing lastDateActive, unless quarantined.
4. Workers missing from a successful complete listWorkers observation transition out of listed availability; failed or partial observations do not create false transitions and remain coverage gaps.
5. Existing recent_contact behavior is unchanged for other pools.
6. Utilization API responses include availability_mode.
7. Pre-cutover Android history cannot be mistaken for listed-mode history.
8. Utilization API docs explain mode semantics and the dormant-versus-dead limitation.
9. Dashboard and pool detail pages disclose listed mode and its limitation.
10. Unit, storage, API, and web-page tests cover both policies, quarantine handling, disappearance, collection failure, metadata, and disclosure text.
